Title: Nobu Toronto - POS Network Issues
Description:
Hello Cybertek and Threatspike Teams,

I am reaching out in hopes of finding a solution to a pesky issue we have
been experiencing with Nobu Toronto's POS system. They are reporting laggy
behavior on the POS workstations, and the CPUs seem to be running at 100%
during their busy hours, causing the POS workstations to freeze up.

My suspicion is that the combination of our N-Central, WorldVue's Kaseya
Agent, and Threatspike are contributing to this behavior. I am wondering if
we could try the following:

*Request for Threatspike: Exclude Simphony installation directories and
database files from real-time scanning. *

*Simphony File Paths: *

Title: Request for Network/DNS/Firewall Review – OPERA Cloud Connectivity Issues on 10.11.10.8 (NHBRC108)
Description:
Hello team,
We are currently experiencing intermittent connectivity issues between our Oracle IFC environment (10.11.10.8 NHBRC108) and OPERA Cloud, and after reviewing logs and working with Oracle support, we have identified that the issue is likely related to network, DNS resolution, or firewall configuration rather than the application layer itself.
Below is a summary of the key findings:
    Multiple instances of:
  *

  *   “Connection closed by remote host”
  *   “Invalid link status – LinkAlive can’t be sent”

  *

  *
Errors indicating failure to reach OPERA Cloud endpoint:

  *   DNS resolution failure (“The remote name could not be resolved”)
  *   SSL/TLS handshake failure (“Could not create SSL/TLS secure channel”)
  *   HTTP 503 Service Unavailable

  *

  *
Environment is using Akamai (Oracle Cloud CDN / security layer), which implies dynamic IP resolution.

Preliminary Conclusion
The issue appears to be caused by intermittent inability to resolve or securely connect to the OPERA Cloud endpoint, likely due to:

  *   DNS inconsistencies or failures
  *   Firewall restrictions (possibly IP-based filtering instead of FQDN)
  *   SSL inspection or TLS interference
  *   Network components (firewall/router) closing sessions prematurely

We kindly ask you to review and validate the following points:

  1.  DNS Resolution
     *   Ensure the OPERA Cloud endpoint (FQDN) resolves consistently from the server
     *   Confirm there are no intermittent DNS failures or caching issues
  2.  Firewall Configuration
     *   Verify that outbound connections are allowed based on FQDN (not static IPs), as OPERA Cloud uses Akamai with dynamic IPs
     *   Confirm that no IP-based restrictions are blocking traffic
  3.  SSL / TLS Inspection
     *   Check if SSL inspection is enabled for outbound traffic
     *   Ensure it is not interfering with secure connections to OPERA Cloud
  4.  Outbound Connectivity
     *   Confirm that required ports (notably 5070 and 5501) are open for outbound communication
  5.  Akamai / CDN Handling
     *   Ensure that traffic to Akamai-backed services is not being restricted or filtered incorrectly
  6.  Session Handling
     *   Verify that no network component (firewall, proxy, etc.) is prematurely terminating sessions

Additional Context

  *   Local services, ports, and interfaces on the Simphony/IFC server are functioning correctly
  *
The issue manifests as intermittent connection drops, not a complete outage
  *
This behavior is impacting downstream systems such as in-room dining integrations, room keys and Symphony Postings.

Please let us know your findings or if you require any additional logs or test results from our side.
Thank you in advance for your support.
Kind regards,
Agustín

Dear Pique Support Team,

During a recent audit of restricted websites, I discovered that I was able to access the following sites on my computer using both Google Chrome and Microsoft Edge:
- OnlyFans
- DraftKings
- Playboy and Hustlers

To verify these findings, I conducted the same test on two additional computers in the accounting department. Could you please advise on the appropriate steps to address this issue? I appreciate your assistance in resolving this matter.

Thank you.

Subject: Re: XNPOS - Embassy Suites Gulf Shores

Good afternoon,

I hate to bother you all on such a beautiful weekend, but our POS system is giving us network issues again. I reported to XNPOS through email and phone that none of our server checks are coming through at the bar. I called first, then I sent the error code through email in which they told me that this is our network providers issue not theirs. I have done a hard reset on the bar printer, and I've also disconnected and reconnected the network cord at the bar. Inside the tech room, all of the network cords seem to be plugged in properly. This is the only printer that is not working. We will try to re-route them for the weekend to fix this issue but I want to make you aware.


Thanks for all of your help!


Clint DeJarnett | Director of Operations F&B

WatchGuard Wisdom from Our Trainers

This week’s technical tip is from Andre Isaza

It is critical for network administrators to understand the traffic that passes through the network and be able to identify the reasons for events. The WatchGuard Firebox provides great tools like Traffic Monitor to help with these tasks, but most people stick to simple search terms such as deny, icmp, or admd. When you’re investigating something more complex, use regular expressions to look for multiple search terms at once.
   
  Learn more about regular expressions in the WatchGuard Help Center.

Title: Request to Expand VLAN 1242 Subnet
Description:
Hello Team,
Due to operational requirements, we need VLAN 1242, which is currently configured as a /24 network, to be expanded to a /23 subnet so that we can have a larger pool of available IP addresses.
We do not require DHCP to be enabled, as all devices connected to this VLAN will use static IP addresses.
Once the devices are ready, we will provide the MAC addresses for each of them. These devices will connect to the access points in the guest rooms, and the MAC addresses will need to be added to the clear list for this VLAN.
Please let us know if you need any additional information from our side.

Title: Introducing the Organization status
Description:
Dear VIG_escalation_contact,
 
At SentinelOne, we know that protecting organizations requires more than just industry-leading AI, it requires a partner that customers can trust. We’ve listened to your feedback and we’re excited to introduce Organization status, a new page providing near real-time visibility into the health of specific SentinelOne environments.
 
Organization status provides visibility into:

	- Instance-specific Information: View the health of specific consoles across all cloud regions in a single, authenticated view.
	- Insights: Track incident start and end times, as well as resolution status for any console you/your customers have access to.
	- Centralized History: Access a 7-day incident log to upcoming and in-progress maintenance windows.


We’ve developed Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs) for Sell partners [https://link.edgepilot.com/s/10937bf8/-rSoXen--UKdHH02oc6I1A?u=https://partners.sentinelone.com/prm/api/objects/v1/asset/tqvbnr7flf3l/_view] as well as Manage & Deliver partners [https://link.edgepilot.com/s/e4623276/qPZvueqFQ0m2yE_dvbtTiw?u=https://partners.sentinelone.com/prm/api/objects/v1/asset/chp2wzpw7img/_view] to help you get started and support your customers. 

Let us know if you have any questions.
 
Regards,
SentinelOne Partner Success

Title: Set up 4 laptops with our GSuite Default and RFMS access
Description:
Good morning,

We went on a recent hiring spree and have 4 new employees starting in the
next week to two.  We ordered 4 laptops today that should be arriving
sometime tomorrow.

They are all 4 Lenovo's: 
Lenovo LOQ RTX 5050 Gaming Laptop, 15.6" FHD 144Hz, AMD Ryzen 7 250, 16GB
DDR5 RAM, 512GB SSD, Backlit Keyboard, RJ-45, Wi-Fi 6, Bundle with PCO
Laptop Cooler, Grey, Windows 11 Home

 

The names of the laptops will need to be:

Sales011

Sales012

Sales013

Admin010

 

They will all need to be set up the Premier Flooring Standard way, with RFMS
Windows App, default to Chrome and have GSuite open on startup, Sentinel 1
and Kaysea.  The Sales013 will also need RFMS measure installed on it as
well as everything else.

None of the laptops should have a password to access them.  They should all
just open with direct access and the 'user' should be named the same as the
PC name.

Admin010 will be traveling from location to location and will need to be
able to print at any location without an issues with print drivers.

 

If they are delivered to us early enough tomorrow, we would like our runner
to come over and drop them off for setup tomorrow.  Otherwise we will send
him on Friday morning with the laptops.

We will need one of them back to us by Th. 3/12 at noon, the second on by
Fr. 3/13 at noon and the other two by the 17th.  Obviously, we will take
them sooner if they are ready.  We will send our runner to pick them up as
they are complete.

Please let me know what questions you have and if there is an issue with
this laptop accommodating what we need.  Users will likely have multiple
chrome tabs open at once all day as well as RFMS.

 

Thank you,
